operation: First, initialize a workplane on the XY plane, then create a solid rectangular box with dimensions of 30 units length, 30 units width, and 10 units height. Next, select the top (highest Z coordinate) face of the box, establish a new workplane on this face, then offset the workplane origin 10 units along the X axis and 10 units along the Y axis. Finally, cut a through counterbore hole at this offset origin, with a 5 unit diameter through bore, 10 unit diameter counterbore, and 4 unit counterbore depth.
geometry: The model is a short square-base rectangular prism, with a single counterbore hole running vertically through its full thickness. The wider counterbore recess is situated on the top square face of the prism, positioned off-center near one corner of the top surface, with a narrower cylindrical through hole extending from the bottom of the counterbore through the rest of the prism.
function: This is a compact square mounting plate or spacer designed for flush fastening. The counterbore hole allows the head of a mounting bolt to sit below the level of the plate's top surface, eliminating fastener protrusion when the part is secured to a supporting surface.
